title: EmbeddingGemma: Powerful and Lightweight Text Representations
eval_comments: evaluated on MTEB across multilingual English and code domains. 300M params designed for low-latency and high-throughput on-device applications. mentions quantization robustness and embedding truncation but no actual latency or throughput numbers reported - memory (300M params and quantization) but claims compute and energy without benchmarking them

title: Falcon-H1: A Family of Hybrid-Head Language Models Redefining Efficiency and Performance
eval_comments: prefill throughput and generation throughput benchmarks reported. hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture for inference efficiency - memory compute and energy

title: GLM-130B: An Open Bilingual Pre-trained Model
eval_comments: multilingual is only English and Chinese. checked efficiency by quantizing to INT4 to fit on RTX 3090/2080 Ti - memory and compute

title: Mixtral of Experts
languages: multilingual (fr de es it and others)
eval_comments: evaluated on multilingual benchmarks and outperforms Llama 2 70B on multilingual. sparse MoE uses only 13B active out of 46.7B total parameters per forward pass. no latency or throughput benchmarks reported - memory only (parameter efficiency via sparsity)

title: Qwen3 Embedding: Advancing Text Embedding and Reranking Through Foundation Models
eval_comments: evaluated on MTEB multilingual text embedding and cross-lingual retrieval. no efficiency benchmarks - just parameter efficiency with multiple model sizes which is memory only

title: Qwen2 Technical Report
languages: en zh es fr de ar ru ko ja th vi and 27+ more
eval_comments: evaluated on multilingual benchmarks across ~30 languages. offers models from 0.5B to 72B with quantization and deployment resources provided. no latency or throughput benchmarks reported - memory only (parameter sizes and quantization support)

title: SeaLLMs - Large Language Models for Southeast Asia
languages: th vi id ms km lo my tl
eval_comments: evaluated on SEA linguistic tasks and instruction-following. outperforms ChatGPT-3.5 on non-Latin languages (Thai Khmer Lao Burmese). described as lightweight and cost-effective but no actual latency or throughput benchmarks - memory only (13B parameters)

title: Qwen2.5 Technical Report
eval_comments: evaluated on wide range of multilingual benchmarks. offers models from 0.5B to 72B with quantized versions available. MoE variants (Qwen2.5-Turbo/Plus) for cost-effectiveness. 72B-Instruct matches Llama-3-405B performance at 5x smaller. no latency or throughput benchmarks reported - memory only (parameter sizes and quantization)

title: Phi-3 Technical Report: A Highly Capable Language Model Locally on Your Phone
eval_comments: tested quantized model on iPhone 14 with A16 Bionic chip running natively on-device and fully offline achieving more than 12 tokens per second. multilingual benchmarks via phi-3.5 series - memory compute and energy

title: PaLM 2 Technical Report
eval_comments: evaluated on English and multilingual language and reasoning tasks including BIG-Bench. claims faster and more efficient inference than PaLM and improved compute efficiency enabling broader deployment. no actual latency or throughput numbers reported - compute and memory (architecture-level efficiency claims only)

title: Yi: Open Foundation Models by 01.AI
eval_comments: bilingual English and Chinese with 6B and 34B models. highlights 4-bit quantization results showing minimal performance drop as foundation for serving on consumer-grade devices. no latency or throughput benchmarks - memory only (quantization for fitting in RAM)

title: Tiny Aya: Bridging Scale and Multilingual Depth
eval_comments: ran MLX-converted model on iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 13. reports prefill and decode throughput (tokens/s) for 100 input and 100 output tokens. iPhone 13 as baseline for large installed base - memory compute and energy

title: Gemma 3 Technical Report
eval_comments: mentions models designed for phones laptops and high-end GPUs but no actual on-device experiments. 1B to 27B parameter range - memory only (just parameter size claims no actual benchmarks)

title: TranslateGemma Technical Report
eval_comments: smaller TranslateGemma models (12B) can match larger baseline Gemma 3 (27B) but no actual latency or throughput benchmarks - memory only (just parameter size comparison)

title: No Language Left Behind: Scaling Human-Centered Machine Translation
eval_comments: evaluated extensively on multilingual translation benchmarks across 200+ languages. no efficiency benchmarks reported - no memory compute or energy evaluation

title: The Llama 3 Herd of Models
eval_comments: evaluated on multilingual benchmarks. also looked at quantization impact on performance and inference efficiency - memory and compute

title: EuroLLM: Multilingual Language Models for Europe
eval_comments: evaluated on multilingual general benchmarks and machine translation for European languages. 1.7B parameter model. no efficiency benchmarks reported - no memory compute or energy evaluation beyond parameter count

title: MaLA-500: Massive Language Adaptation of Large Language Models
eval_comments: evaluated on SIB-200 and other multilingual benchmarks across 534 languages via vocabulary extension and continued pretraining on LLaMA 2. no efficiency benchmarks reported - no memory compute or energy evaluation

title: Phi-4-Mini Technical Report: Compact yet Powerful Multimodal Language Models via Mixture-of-LoRAs
languages: en de es fr it ja pt zh (8 for speech); multilingual text via 200K vocab
eval_comments: evaluated on Multilingual-MMLU and MGSM for multilingual text plus CommonVoice and FLEURS ASR across 8 languages. GQA reduces KV cache by 1/3 but no latency or throughput benchmarks - memory only

title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3: Efficient and Open Intelligence
eval_comments: throughput benchmarks show 3.3x higher than Qwen3-30B-A3B. accuracy-efficiency tradeoff curves with reasoning budget control. NVFP4 training with <1% loss gap. but NO multilingual benchmarks - all English evals only (MMLU AIME coding math) - memory compute and energy

title: Granite 3.0 Language Models
languages: en de es fr ja pt ar cs it ko nl zh
eval_comments: evaluated on PAWS-X (7 langs) and MGSM (6 langs) for multilingual. reports training energy consumption and carbon emissions (GPU-hours MWh tCO2eq). mentions edge devices mobile phones smart watches but no inference latency or throughput benchmarks - memory and energy (training-side only)
